stories.title: Iran strikes caused $400M in damage to US Navy base, WSJ finds
stories.description: The Pentagon is now weighing relocating some Gulf installations to Israel and moving command centers underground, according to the investigation.

stories.title: Israel-Lebanon talks extend into Friday over withdrawal deadlock
stories.description: Lebanon demands a full Israeli pullout with a timeline, but Netanyahu has pledged Israeli forces are "not going to withdraw."

stories.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll surpasses 500 as global aid arrives
stories.description: Acting President Delcy Rodríguez said Friday the toll from Wednesday's twin quakes more than doubled overnight, with thousands still missing.

stories.title: Crimea declares state of emergency amid massive Ukrainian drone campaign
stories.description: Russian-installed authorities invoked emergency powers as drone strikes cripple fuel supplies, power, and tourism across the occupied peninsula.

stories.title: SpaceX plans Starlink mobile service to rival U.S. wireless carriers
stories.description: Gwynne Shotwell told IPO roadshow investors that SpaceX could sell mobile contracts directly to consumers and build its own terrestrial network.

stories.title: Judge lets lawsuit against Trump's $1.8B fund proceed, questions DOJ's 'trustworthiness'
stories.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday the DOJ's refusal to submit a sworn statement confirming the fund's closure keeps the legal challenge alive.

stories.title: U.S. eases Venezuela sanctions to speed earthquake relief
stories.description: The Treasury Department authorized relief transactions through October after back-to-back quakes killed at least 188 people.

stories.title: NYC approves historic rent freeze on stabilized apartments
stories.description: The Rent Guidelines Board voted Thursday to freeze rents on roughly one million apartments, delivering on Mayor Mamdani's central campaign promise.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill 3 in Lebanon as ceasefire talks wrap in Washington
stories.description: Drone attacks and border clashes left an Israeli soldier and three Lebanese civilians dead Thursday, even as U.S.-mediated negotiations entered their final day.

stories.title: Speaker Johnson visits White House to resolve housing bill standoff with Trump
stories.description: Trump canceled the signing of the landmark housing affordability bill, demanding Congress first pass the SAVE America Act voting restrictions measure.

stories.title: NY prosecutors drop Weinstein rape charge after accuser declines to testify again
stories.description: Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg's office dismissed the third-degree rape charge after Jessica Mann said she "can no longer go through this."

stories.title: US tests Ukrainian Magura naval drones in Philippines
stories.description: The combat-proven Magura V5 sank a target ship during Balikatan 2026, marking its first use outside the Russia-Ukraine war.

stories.title: Cargo ship struck in Strait of Hormuz amid Iran threats
stories.description: UKMTO reported a cargo vessel was hit by an unknown projectile Thursday, hours after Iran's IRGC warned ships against using a new Omani corridor.

stories.title: Supreme Court rules Trump can end TPS for Haitians, Syrians
stories.description: The 6-3 decision strips protections from over 350,000 immigrants and could affect up to 1.3 million TPS holders nationwide.

stories.title: Supreme Court rules federal law blocks Roundup cancer lawsuits
stories.description: The decision shields **Bayer** from thousands of claims alleging the company should have labeled the herbicide as a potential carcinogen.

stories.title: Macron says Europe and US in 'reconvergence' on Ukraine
stories.description: Europe's five biggest military powers met in Berlin on Wednesday to coordinate ahead of next month's NATO summit in Ankara.

stories.title: Supreme Court lets Trump turn back asylum seekers at border
stories.description: The 6-3 ruling in Mullin v. Al Otro Lado holds that migrants standing in Mexico have not "arrived" in the U.S. under federal law.

stories.title: 6.9 magnitude earthquake strikes off northeastern Japan
stories.description: The quake hit near Kuji, Iwate Prefecture, on Thursday morning local time, prompting a tsunami warning for the region.

stories.title: Wright says U.S. can keep Hormuz open without Iran deal
stories.description: Oil transit through the Strait of Hormuz has returned to pre-war volume levels, with 72 vessels carrying 20 million barrels in 24 hours.

stories.title: Senate reverses Iran war powers vote after Trump confrontation
stories.description: Two Republican senators changed their positions late Wednesday after a shouting match between Trump and GOP lawmakers at the Capitol.

stories.title: Army, Navy, Air Force restore flu shot mandate for recruits after base outbreak
stories.description: The Pentagon granted exceptions to Hegseth's voluntary vaccine policy after 222 trainees fell ill at Lackland Air Force Base.

stories.title: ICC bureau recommends firing prosecutor Karim Khan
stories.description: Details of the June 8 decision emerged Tuesday, revealing findings of "non-consensual sexual contact" with a junior staff member.

stories.title: Trump orders DOJ to probe Exxon, Chevron over gas prices
stories.description: The president accused major oil companies of "gouging" consumers by keeping pump prices high despite falling crude costs after the Iran peace deal.

stories.title: Rubio says U.S.-Iran talks to resume in Switzerland June 29
stories.description: The secretary of state, on a Gulf tour to reassure allies, also rejected any Iranian tolls on the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Venezuela set to reveal $240B debt in largest restructuring
stories.description: The figure far exceeds prior market estimates of $150 billion to $200 billion, according to the Financial Times.

stories.title: Federal judge permanently blocks Trump's elections executive order
stories.description: Chief U.S. District Judge Denise Casper in Boston converted her 2025 preliminary injunction into a permanent ban, joining two other judges who struck down the order.

stories.title: OpenAI unveils Jalapeño, its first custom AI chip
stories.description: Developed with Broadcom, the inference chip rivals Nvidia's Blackwell processors, according to Broadcom CEO Hock Tan.

stories.title: China defends right to target people abroad under new ethnic unity law
stories.description: Vice Justice Minister Hu Weilie dismissed "long-arm jurisdiction" criticism, saying the law taking effect July 1 is "legitimate and lawful."

stories.title: Ex-Adams chief of staff Frank Carone arrested by FBI
stories.description: Carone and three others face a federal bribery case tied to a city contract issued during the Adams administration.

stories.title: U.S. and Iran clash over nuclear deal terms days after signing
stories.description: Trump insists Iran agreed to indefinite nuclear inspections; Tehran denies its nuclear program was even discussed during Swiss talks.

stories.title: US warns 'time is not on Moscow's side' as peace talks stall
stories.description: Putin and Lavrov insisted on Moscow's terms for Ukraine negotiations as the U.S. called for an immediate ceasefire at the UN Security Council.

stories.title: Mamdani-backed candidates sweep three NYC congressional primaries
stories.description: The New York mayor's progressive slate toppled incumbents and establishment Democrats in Tuesday's primary races across the city.

stories.title: Kim commissions North Korea's largest warship, pledges nuclear navy
stories.description: The 5,000-ton destroyer Choe Hyon entered service Tuesday after 14 months of testing, as Kim outlined plans for 10,000-ton warships.

stories.title: Federal judge bars ICE arrests at immigration courts nationwide
stories.description: The nationwide injunction vacates Trump-era policies and reinstates Biden-era limits on courthouse arrests and short-term detention.

stories.title: Alan Wilson wins South Carolina GOP governor runoff
stories.description: Wilson defeated Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette by a wide margin after Trump hedged his endorsement by backing both candidates.

stories.title: Meta is building a prediction markets app to rival Polymarket and Kalshi
stories.description: CEO Mark Zuckerberg directed a team to develop "Arena," a standalone app that would initially use a points system instead of real money.

stories.title: Trump says Reflecting Pool will be drained again for 'permanent repair' around July 4
stories.description: The announcement comes as algae and peeling blue paint plague the $14 million renovation, while the White House ballroom's cost has tripled to $600 million.

stories.title: DOJ, New York file dueling lawsuits over mask ban for federal agents
stories.description: New York's law barring officers from wearing face coverings takes effect Friday, setting up an urgent legal clash over immigration enforcement.

stories.title: Bennett says Israel smuggled Starlink into Iran
stories.description: Former PM told a Jerusalem summit he initiated covert delivery of tens of thousands of satellite receivers to help Iranian protesters.

stories.title: DOJ charges 455 in $6.5 billion healthcare fraud takedown
stories.description: The annual crackdown includes a record number of Medicaid fraud defendants and targets schemes involving false billing, opioid distribution, and wound care fraud.

stories.title: Appeals court clears Trump to expand fast-track deportations nationwide
stories.description: A D.C. Circuit panel reversed a lower court's block on expedited removal, allowing deportations without hearings for undocumented immigrants found anywhere in the U.S.

stories.title: US, Iran finalize deal to release $12B in frozen assets
stories.description: Iran's chief negotiator says the agreement, reached in Switzerland, will release the funds in two $6 billion tranches.

stories.title: Tucker Carlson says he's 'out' of the Republican Party
stories.description: The conservative commentator cited the GOP's alignment with Israel and support for the Iran war as reasons he can no longer back the party.

stories.title: Supreme Court lets border agents strip green card holders' status over unproven charges
stories.description: Justice Jackson warned the 6-3 ruling hands the government a "massive blank check" to put returning legal residents into immigration limbo.

stories.title: Trump Jr. met Adani in India before DOJ dropped charges
stories.description: Bloomberg reported that the previously undisclosed meeting took place in Ahmedabad last November, while Adani faced U.S. bribery charges later dismissed in May.

stories.title: Mexico seeks to restart oil shipments to Cuba amid crisis
stories.description: President Sheinbaum said Mexico will work to resume fuel deliveries as Cuba faces a humanitarian emergency with hospitals and schools shutting down.

stories.title: Supreme Court sides with Cisco, ends Falun Gong suit
stories.description: The 6-3 ruling bars federal courts from creating new causes of action under the Alien Tort Statute for international human rights violations.

stories.title: ODNI firings begin as Democrats warn acting DNI Pulte
stories.description: CNN reported Monday that layoffs at the intelligence office started even as top lawmakers cautioned Pulte against sweeping personnel cuts.

stories.title: Coast Guard helicopter crashes near Sitka, Alaska
stories.description: All four crew members were transported to a local hospital; the extent of their injuries is not yet known.

stories.title: Supreme Court bars Rastafarian from suing guards who shaved his dreadlocks
stories.description: The 6-3 ruling found that a federal religious rights law does not allow prisoners to seek money damages from state officials personally.

stories.title: US Treasury issues 60-day license for Iranian oil sales
stories.description: The sweeping sanctions waiver permits production, delivery, and U.S. import of Iranian crude through August 21 as part of a broader deal framework.

stories.title: Starmer resigns as UK prime minister after two years
stories.description: Andy Burnham, who won a special election last week, is the frontrunner to succeed him as Labour leader and PM.

stories.title: Judge blocks Trump's use of SAVE database for voter checks
stories.description: The ruling halts a key pillar of Trump's election integrity push days before a June 30 deadline to begin sharing citizenship data with states.

stories.title: Judge blocks DOJ subpoenas targeting Walz over immigration
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Patrick Schiltz ruled the grand jury subpoenas were issued to "harass and retaliate" against Minnesota officials who refused to aid federal immigration enforcement.

stories.title: Alan Greenspan, Fed chairman for 19 years, dies at 100
stories.description: His wife, Andrea Mitchell, said he died Monday morning from complications of Parkinson's disease at their home.

stories.title: China sanctions 56 US firms over Pentagon blacklistings
stories.description: Beijing placed 10 American companies on its export control list and barred 46 others from government procurement in retaliation.

stories.title: Supreme Court reinstates murder conviction in Etan Patz case
stories.description: The 6-3 ruling ends years of legal uncertainty over the 1979 disappearance that reshaped the missing-children movement in America.

stories.title: US, Canada, Mexico set July 1 trilateral meeting to review USMCA
stories.description: The first formal three-way review session comes as Trump has said he is "not interested in renewing" the trade pact he negotiated during his first term.

stories.title: EU leaders commit to 'decisively' ramping up defense by 2030
stories.description: The Brussels summit also saw clashes over the bloc's long-term budget and debate on tougher trade measures against China.

stories.title: At least 30 die in Congo displacement camp as Ebola spreads undetected
stories.description: Families in the Kigonze camp have refused Ebola testing on patients and the deceased, hampering efforts to contain the Bundibugyo strain outbreak.

stories.title: Trump says Cuba operation could mirror Venezuela raid
stories.description: The president told Axios that action against Havana is "possible" while praising Secretary of State Rubio's role in talks with Cuba.

stories.title: Israeli envoy clashes with UN officials over child violations report
stories.description: A record 38,558 grave violations against children in conflict were verified in 2025, with government forces identified as the main perpetrators for the first time.

stories.title: Iran eyes $105B annual oil windfall as US deal lifts export barriers
stories.description: Tehran has also laid groundwork to charge transit fees on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz once a 60-day grace period ends.

stories.title: Trump unveils Qatar-gifted jet as new Air Force One
stories.description: The aircraft was shown at Joint Base Andrews hours after the iconic VC-25A completed its final presidential mission following 35 years of service.

stories.title: Iran agrees to invite IAEA to inspect nuclear sites, Witkoff tells Congress
stories.description: U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff briefed lawmakers Thursday that Tehran will also allow American nuclear inspectors to visit under the deal signed this week.

stories.title: Vance warns Israel not to alienate its 'only powerful ally' over Iran deal
stories.description: The vice president told Israeli cabinet critics to "wake up and smell the reality" as Lebanon emerges as a flashpoint in the U.S.-Iran agreement.

stories.title: Macron says Trump changed his mind on Ukraine after G7
stories.description: The French president said Trump arrived at the Evian summit believing Ukraine would lose but left with a shifted assessment.

stories.title: Supreme Court rules marijuana users can own guns
stories.description: The justices sided with a Texas man who challenged a federal law barring drug users from possessing firearms as a Second Amendment violation.

stories.title: EU pushes for role in US-Iran talks after MOU signing
stories.description: The 14-point memorandum of understanding starts a 60-day clock for nuclear negotiations, but Europe has no formal seat at the table.

stories.title: Trump open to phased approach on North Korea, Lee says
stories.description: South Korea's president said Trump agreed to consider prioritizing a nuclear freeze over immediate denuclearization after their G7 talks.

stories.title: Hegseth announces six-month review of U.S. forces in Europe
stories.description: The defense secretary criticized NATO allies for denying base access during Iran operations and demanded Europe take primary responsibility for its own defense.

stories.title: NASA partners with Eric Schmidt's Relativity Space on 2028 Mars orbiter
stories.description: The Aeolus mission will pair NASA atmospheric instruments with Relativity Space's Terran R rocket to study Martian winds, dust, and climate.

stories.title: Israel calls Trump's Iran deal a 'catastrophic capitulation'
stories.description: Analysts and officials across the political spectrum condemned the 14-point memorandum signed at Versailles, saying it leaves Iran's nuclear and missile programs unaddressed.

stories.title: Georgia GOP scraps redistricting plans ahead of special session
stories.description: House Republicans cited pending litigation and pulled map-redrawing from Wednesday's agenda, fearing it would energize Democrats before November.

stories.title: Gilgo Beach killer sentenced to life without parole
stories.description: Judge called Rex Heuermann "a disgusting, small man" and "a coward" before ordering him removed from the courtroom to cheers.

stories.title: ICE to sell or give away 7 detention warehouses it bought for $700M
stories.description: New DHS Secretary Markwayne Mullin is reversing Kristi Noem's mega-detention strategy, shifting to existing facilities amid legal challenges and overspending scrutiny.

stories.title: Von der Leyen signals no third term as EU chief
stories.description: Her chief of cabinet shared the message at a private dinner to quell concerns about a planned restructuring of Commission departments.

stories.title: G7 summit wraps up with pledges on debt, Ebola, development reform
stories.description: Leaders in Évian-les-Bains issued joint declarations urging a shift from aid dependency to private investment and a coordinated Ebola response.

stories.title: Trump vows to block FISA unless voting bill attached
stories.description: Speaking at the G7 summit, the president demanded Congress link his SAVE America Act to the lapsed surveillance law renewal.

stories.title: Leak reveals members of Thiel's secretive Dialog society
stories.description: Internal records exposed online identify over 200 powerful figures in politics, tech, and finance tied to the invite-only network.

stories.title: Brazil's top court convicts Eduardo Bolsonaro for coercion
stories.description: The former lawmaker was sentenced to over four years in prison for lobbying the U.S. to intervene in his father's coup trial.

stories.title: Senate blocks bid to overturn Trump immigration appeals rule
stories.description: Senators voted 46-48 to reject a procedural motion on the resolution, leaving the fast-track deportation appeals process in place.

stories.title: Federal judge blocks Idaho transgender bathroom law before July 1 start
stories.description: Chief U.S. District Judge Amanda Brailsford ruled the law is likely unconstitutionally vague, halting enforcement statewide.

stories.title: Russian frigate fires warning shots at yacht in English Channel
stories.description: The UK Ministry of Defence is investigating after the Admiral Grigorovich discharged rounds near a civilian vessel south of the Isle of Wight.

stories.title: Key GOP swing vote signals optimism on Blanche AG pick
stories.description: Sen. Tillis, seen as the critical vote on the Judiciary Committee, said he is "generally satisfied" with the acting attorney general's paperwork.

stories.title: 15 charged in Minnesota for allegedly impeding ICE agents
stories.description: Prosecutors say the defendants stalked agents, threw ice at convoys, and blockaded federal buildings during a major immigration crackdown.

stories.title: Pew: 52% of two-parent families now have both working full time
stories.description: Two-thirds of working mothers say they cannot give 100% at home, and over half say the same about work, a new survey finds.

stories.title: Trump shifts special ed, civil rights out of Education Dept.
stories.description: The Office of Special Education moves to HHS and the Office for Civil Rights to the DOJ, furthering efforts to dismantle the department.

stories.title: FBI foils alleged drone attack plot targeting White House UFC event
stories.description: Five suspects are in custody after authorities say they disrupted a multi-phase explosive drone scheme aimed at the UFC Freedom 250 card.

stories.title: SpaceX to buy Cursor maker Anysphere for $60B
stories.description: Days after its record Nasdaq IPO, SpaceX said it will exercise its option to acquire the AI coding startup in an all-stock deal.

stories.title: Trump invokes Defense Production Act to boost munitions output
stories.description: The June 11 memo, made public Tuesday, directs the Pentagon to compel manufacturers to prioritize weapons production amid the Iran conflict.

stories.title: DOJ calls xAI vital to national security in bid to dismiss NAACP pollution suit
stories.description: The Justice Department argued Monday that xAI's unpermitted gas turbines in Mississippi support military operations and should not be shut down.

stories.title: Israel vows to hold Lebanon territory indefinitely, defying US-Iran peace deal
stories.description: Defense Minister Katz said Israeli forces will not withdraw from seized lands in Lebanon, Syria, or Gaza, challenging the framework agreement.

stories.title: Thune demands briefing on Iran deal before Geneva signing
stories.description: Senate Republicans are withholding support for Trump's framework agreement with Iran, insisting Congress review and vote on the deal.

stories.title: Skydiving plane crash kills all 12 on board in Missouri
stories.description: The plane had just taken off from Butler Memorial Airport when it lost power, stalled, and went down nose-first, the acting airport manager said.

stories.title: Sweden scrambles jets to intercept Russian aircraft twice in one day
stories.description: The Friday interceptions over the Baltic Sea came a day after Sweden's defense commission warned Russia may test NATO's cohesion soon.

stories.title: Trump cabinet allies push kratom agenda amid financial ties, NYT details
stories.description: Two Trump cabinet members advanced kratom industry interests while one held up to $1 million in a kratom company, the Times reported.

stories.title: UN reports Ukraine's May civilian toll highest in 4 years
stories.description: At least 274 civilians were killed and 1,763 injured, a 93% increase over May 2025, according to UN monitors.

stories.title: UAE agreed to unlock billions for Iran, sources say
stories.description: Reuters reported that the UAE agreed to release up to $10 billion to halt Iranian attacks, though the UAE categorically denied the claims.

stories.title: Trump White House secretly weighed suspending habeas corpus, book reveals
stories.description: Secret memos show officials debated limiting immigrants' habeas corpus rights more extensively than previously known, according to a New York Times report.

stories.title: State Attorneys General open multistate investigation into OpenAI
stories.description: New York's attorney general served OpenAI with a sweeping subpoena on behalf of a coalition of states, targeting user data practices and impact on minors.

stories.title: DOJ approves Paramount's $111 billion Warner Bros. Discovery merger
stories.description: The deal cleared federal antitrust review without any required divestitures or concessions, though state attorneys general may still sue to block it.

stories.title: Trump aides fear reporters obtained Situation Room tapes
stories.description: Axios reports White House officials suspect Haberman and Swan acquired secret audio from sensitive meetings for their book "Regime Change," due June 23.

stories.title: Elon Musk becomes the world’s first trillionaire after SpaceX IPO
stories.description: The rocket and satellite company raised $75 billion by selling shares at $135 each, valuing SpaceX at $1.77 trillion at its Nasdaq debut.

stories.title: EU says it has proof China trained Russian troops fighting in Ukraine
stories.description: European intelligence confirmed that hundreds of Russian soldiers trained at Chinese military bases before deploying to the front lines.

stories.title: Trump marks 80th birthday with UFC fights on White House lawn
stories.description: The president hosts UFC Freedom 250 on the South Lawn as polls show growing public concern over his health and mental acuity.

stories.title: Pentagon releases third batch of declassified UFO files
stories.description: The latest tranche includes 72 documents, videos, and NASA audio recordings spanning back to 1949, with new firsthand accounts of luminous "orbs."

stories.title: Bezos and Amodei offer clashing visions of AI's job impact
stories.description: AI-linked U.S. layoffs hit a record monthly high in May even as Bezos predicts the technology will create labor shortages, not eliminate jobs.

stories.title: Charlie Javice seeks Trump pardon for JPMorgan fraud conviction
stories.description: The Frank founder, sentenced to 85 months for fabricating customer data to sell her startup for $175 million, has reached out to Trump allies.

stories.title: U.S. plans to cut fighter jets, warships for NATO in Europe
stories.description: A New York Times report details sweeping reductions to aircraft, submarines, and carriers available for European defense operations.

stories.title: Bezos' AI startup Prometheus raises $12B at $41B valuation
stories.description: The industrial AI company closed its Series B backed by JPMorgan, BlackRock, and Goldman Sachs, bringing total funding to $18.2 billion.

stories.title: Trump admin blocks foreign access to Anthropic's Fable 5, Mythos 5
stories.description: Commerce Secretary Lutnick ordered export restrictions on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5, barring all foreign users days after the models launched.

stories.title: Supreme Court blocks Alabama nitrogen gas execution
stories.description: The 6-3 ruling upheld a lower court's finding that nitrogen hypoxia violates the Eighth Amendment's ban on cruel and unusual punishment.

stories.title: ECB hikes rates for first time in 3 years as Iran war fuels inflation
stories.description: The deposit rate rises to 2.25% as eurozone inflation hits 3.2%, with markets pricing in roughly even odds of another increase in September.

stories.title: Trump says US strike killed Tren de Aragua leader
stories.description: The president announced the killing of Niño Guerrero, who had been indicted on terrorism and racketeering charges and carried a $5 million bounty.

stories.title: Boelter pleads guilty to killing Minnesota lawmaker Hortman
stories.description: The plea deal calls for two life sentences plus 40 years after federal prosecutors agreed not to seek the death penalty.

stories.title: OpenAI considers drastic token price cuts to fend off Anthropic
stories.description: The move, reported by The Wall Street Journal, comes as both AI giants prepare for IPOs and enterprise customers push back on soaring costs.

stories.title: Trump and allies push to void his two impeachments
stories.description: The effort to formally expunge both first-term House impeachment votes from the record would wait until after the November midterm elections.

stories.title: Pentagon locked down after air quality alert triggers hazmat response
stories.description: Emergency crews in full chemical protective gear responded Thursday after internal systems flagged an air quality concern at the military headquarters.

stories.title: IAEA board passes resolution demanding Iran account for uranium stockpile
stories.description: The U.S.-backed measure requires Tehran to disclose the status of enriched uranium at sites bombed during last year's conflict and grant inspectors access.

stories.title: Russia's war in Ukraine surpasses World War I in duration
stories.description: The full-scale invasion reached 1,569 days on Wednesday, outlasting the First World War as the Trump administration's June peace deadline passed without resolution.

stories.title: Hegseth warns Cuba against acquiring weapons during Guantanamo visit
stories.description: The defense secretary told Havana that seeking arms capable of reaching the base or the U.S. homeland would invite a confrontation it "could not withstand."

stories.title: Gates tells Congress Epstein tried to blackmail him
stories.description: The Microsoft co-founder testified before the House Oversight Committee, saying Epstein used knowledge of his affairs to pressure him.

stories.title: Trump administration appeals ruling that struck down $100,000 H-1B visa fee
stories.description: The Justice Department filed a notice of appeal Thursday, escalating a legal fight over the fee a federal judge called an unlawful tax.

stories.title: US strikes destroy water reservoirs in Iran, drawing war crime accusations
stories.description: Iran says US airstrikes on Sirik cut drinking water to thousands, as both sides trade fire in a second day of renewed hostilities.

stories.title: US consumer prices rose 4.2% in May, fastest pace in 3 years
stories.description: The Iran war-driven energy surge pushed headline CPI past 4% for the first time since 2023, though a softer core reading eased fears of broader inflation.

stories.title: White House defends Cuba sanctions after UN warns children are dying
stories.description: The Trump administration said sanctions target the Cuban regime, rejecting UN calls to lift measures that officials say are causing "widespread harm."

stories.title: IRGC claims drone strike on US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain
stories.description: Iran says it attacked the US naval headquarters hours after American strikes hit Iranian military sites in a rapid cycle of retaliation.

stories.title: Fired Navy admiral advances to runoff for Mace's SC House seat
stories.description: Nancy Lacore, dismissed by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, will face Mac Deford in a June 23 Democratic runoff for South Carolina's 1st District.

stories.title: Trump claims US secretly took oil from Iran; Energy Secretary says he was unaware
stories.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright told Congress he had no knowledge of the operation Trump described as extracting "millions of barrels every night."

stories.title: House passes Faster Labor Contracts Act as 20 Republicans break ranks
stories.description: The bipartisan bill would force employers to begin union contract talks within 10 days and impose binding arbitration if negotiations stall.

stories.title: Platner wins Maine Democratic Senate primary, will face Collins in fall
stories.description: The scandal-plagued oyster farmer and veteran secured the nomination decisively, setting up a marquee race for Senate control.

stories.title: Trump signs $70B Secure America Act funding ICE, CBP
stories.description: The law locks in immigration enforcement funding through 2029, ending a months-long standoff that triggered the longest government shutdown in U.S. history.

stories.title: Belfast rocked by riots after Sudanese man charged in stabbing
stories.description: Masked protesters torched vehicles and blocked roads Tuesday night as the suspect, a refugee granted UK asylum in 2023, was charged with attempted murder.

stories.title: Anthropic launches Claude Fable 5, its first public Mythos-class model
stories.description: The AI model uses automatic safety classifiers that route sensitive queries to a less powerful system, with mandatory 30-day data retention.

stories.title: Becerra, Hilton advance to California governor race
stories.description: Republican Steve Hilton clinched the second spot Tuesday, setting up a November showdown with Democrat Xavier Becerra to succeed Gov. Gavin Newsom.

stories.title: US warns Europe to tighten Ebola travel curbs or face restrictions
stories.description: The Trump administration says Europe's inaction on Ebola screening could trigger limits on European travelers ahead of the World Cup.

stories.title: Social Security trust fund now projected to run dry in 2032
stories.description: The 2026 trustees report moves the OASI depletion date up a year, meaning retirees could face a 22% benefit cut as early as late 2032.

stories.title: NASA names four astronauts for Artemis III lunar mission
stories.description: Bresnik, Parmitano, Douglas, and Rubio will fly a 2027 Earth-orbit test flight critical to returning humans to the moon.

stories.title: MAGA Inc. plans $1M-per-person fundraiser before White House UFC event
stories.description: Trump's super PAC is scheduled to hold the dinner Saturday at his Virginia golf club, one day before UFC Freedom 250 on the South Lawn.

stories.title: Houthis strike two ships after banning Israeli Red Sea navigation
stories.description: The attacks enforce a declared ban on Israeli-linked vessels, threatening Saudi Arabia's last functioning oil export route as the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.

stories.title: Israel strikes Tyre, killing 8, defying Iran warning
stories.description: The IDF issued its first evacuation order covering Tyre's Christian quarter as airstrikes hit the southern Lebanese city Tuesday.

stories.title: Drone boat rescues Apache crew in military first after Strait of Hormuz crash
stories.description: A U.S. military sea drone conducted its first-ever water rescue after an AH-64 Apache went down off Oman's coast Monday evening.

stories.title: China plans $295B data center buildout to rival U.S. in AI
stories.description: Beijing is drafting a blueprint for interconnected computing hubs nationwide, funded largely through sovereign debt over the next five years.

stories.title: Philippines earthquake death toll rises to 37 as rescuers search for survivors
stories.description: The 7.8-magnitude quake struck off Mindanao on Monday, triggering landslides, building collapses, and tsunami waves across the region.

stories.title: Watchdog finds flawed data, security gaps in IRS-ICE deal
stories.description: TIGTA's report documents matching errors and ICE's failure to safeguard taxpayer information shared under the controversial 2025 agreement.

stories.title: Senate Democrats seek probe into Duffy's corporate-funded road trip
stories.description: Six senators asked the DOT inspector general to investigate the transportation secretary's family video series bankrolled by regulated companies.

stories.title: Trump to ask Supreme Court to revive $475M CNN suit
stories.description: His lawyers requested a 60-day extension to file a petition challenging lower courts' dismissal of the "Big Lie" defamation case.

stories.title: State Department to offer $750 fast-track visa interviews
stories.description: A pilot program launching July 1 lets B-1/B-2 visa applicants pay $750 on top of the standard $185 fee to secure an interview within 10 business days.

stories.title: 6.1 earthquake off Cuba sends tremors across Florida
stories.description: The quake struck around 2 p.m. Monday about 65 miles from Mantua, Cuba, with shaking reported from the Keys to Jacksonville.

top.title: Iran strikes caused $400M in damage to US Navy base, WSJ finds
top.description: The Pentagon is now weighing relocating some Gulf installations to Israel and moving command centers underground, according to the investigation.

top.title: Israel-Lebanon talks extend into Friday over withdrawal deadlock
top.description: Lebanon demands a full Israeli pullout with a timeline, but Netanyahu has pledged Israeli forces are "not going to withdraw."

top.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll surpasses 500 as global aid arrives
top.description: Acting President Delcy Rodríguez said Friday the toll from Wednesday's twin quakes more than doubled overnight, with thousands still missing.

top.title: Crimea declares state of emergency amid massive Ukrainian drone campaign
top.description: Russian-installed authorities invoked emergency powers as drone strikes cripple fuel supplies, power, and tourism across the occupied peninsula.

top.title: SpaceX plans Starlink mobile service to rival U.S. wireless carriers
top.description: Gwynne Shotwell told IPO roadshow investors that SpaceX could sell mobile contracts directly to consumers and build its own terrestrial network.

top.title: Judge lets lawsuit against Trump's $1.8B fund proceed, questions DOJ's 'trustworthiness'
top.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday the DOJ's refusal to submit a sworn statement confirming the fund's closure keeps the legal challenge alive.

top.title: U.S. eases Venezuela sanctions to speed earthquake relief
top.description: The Treasury Department authorized relief transactions through October after back-to-back quakes killed at least 188 people.

top.title: NYC approves historic rent freeze on stabilized apartments
top.description: The Rent Guidelines Board voted Thursday to freeze rents on roughly one million apartments, delivering on Mayor Mamdani's central campaign promise.

top.title: Israeli strikes kill 3 in Lebanon as ceasefire talks wrap in Washington
top.description: Drone attacks and border clashes left an Israeli soldier and three Lebanese civilians dead Thursday, even as U.S.-mediated negotiations entered their final day.

top.title: Speaker Johnson visits White House to resolve housing bill standoff with Trump
top.description: Trump canceled the signing of the landmark housing affordability bill, demanding Congress first pass the SAVE America Act voting restrictions measure.

top.title: NY prosecutors drop Weinstein rape charge after accuser declines to testify again
top.description: Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg's office dismissed the third-degree rape charge after Jessica Mann said she "can no longer go through this."

top.title: US tests Ukrainian Magura naval drones in Philippines
top.description: The combat-proven Magura V5 sank a target ship during Balikatan 2026, marking its first use outside the Russia-Ukraine war.

top.title: Cargo ship struck in Strait of Hormuz amid Iran threats
top.description: UKMTO reported a cargo vessel was hit by an unknown projectile Thursday, hours after Iran's IRGC warned ships against using a new Omani corridor.

top.title: Supreme Court rules Trump can end TPS for Haitians, Syrians
top.description: The 6-3 decision strips protections from over 350,000 immigrants and could affect up to 1.3 million TPS holders nationwide.

top.title: Supreme Court rules federal law blocks Roundup cancer lawsuits
top.description: The decision shields **Bayer** from thousands of claims alleging the company should have labeled the herbicide as a potential carcinogen.

top.title: Macron says Europe and US in 'reconvergence' on Ukraine
top.description: Europe's five biggest military powers met in Berlin on Wednesday to coordinate ahead of next month's NATO summit in Ankara.

top.title: Supreme Court lets Trump turn back asylum seekers at border
top.description: The 6-3 ruling in Mullin v. Al Otro Lado holds that migrants standing in Mexico have not "arrived" in the U.S. under federal law.

top.title: 6.9 magnitude earthquake strikes off northeastern Japan
top.description: The quake hit near Kuji, Iwate Prefecture, on Thursday morning local time, prompting a tsunami warning for the region.

top.title: Wright says U.S. can keep Hormuz open without Iran deal
top.description: Oil transit through the Strait of Hormuz has returned to pre-war volume levels, with 72 vessels carrying 20 million barrels in 24 hours.

top.title: Senate reverses Iran war powers vote after Trump confrontation
top.description: Two Republican senators changed their positions late Wednesday after a shouting match between Trump and GOP lawmakers at the Capitol.

top.title: Army, Navy, Air Force restore flu shot mandate for recruits after base outbreak
top.description: The Pentagon granted exceptions to Hegseth's voluntary vaccine policy after 222 trainees fell ill at Lackland Air Force Base.

top.title: ICC bureau recommends firing prosecutor Karim Khan
top.description: Details of the June 8 decision emerged Tuesday, revealing findings of "non-consensual sexual contact" with a junior staff member.

top.title: Trump orders DOJ to probe Exxon, Chevron over gas prices
top.description: The president accused major oil companies of "gouging" consumers by keeping pump prices high despite falling crude costs after the Iran peace deal.

top.title: Rubio says U.S.-Iran talks to resume in Switzerland June 29
top.description: The secretary of state, on a Gulf tour to reassure allies, also rejected any Iranian tolls on the Strait of Hormuz.

top.title: Venezuela set to reveal $240B debt in largest restructuring
top.description: The figure far exceeds prior market estimates of $150 billion to $200 billion, according to the Financial Times.

top.title: Federal judge permanently blocks Trump's elections executive order
top.description: Chief U.S. District Judge Denise Casper in Boston converted her 2025 preliminary injunction into a permanent ban, joining two other judges who struck down the order.

top.title: OpenAI unveils Jalapeño, its first custom AI chip
top.description: Developed with Broadcom, the inference chip rivals Nvidia's Blackwell processors, according to Broadcom CEO Hock Tan.

top.title: China defends right to target people abroad under new ethnic unity law
top.description: Vice Justice Minister Hu Weilie dismissed "long-arm jurisdiction" criticism, saying the law taking effect July 1 is "legitimate and lawful."

top.title: Ex-Adams chief of staff Frank Carone arrested by FBI
top.description: Carone and three others face a federal bribery case tied to a city contract issued during the Adams administration.

top.title: U.S. and Iran clash over nuclear deal terms days after signing
top.description: Trump insists Iran agreed to indefinite nuclear inspections; Tehran denies its nuclear program was even discussed during Swiss talks.

top.title: US warns 'time is not on Moscow's side' as peace talks stall
top.description: Putin and Lavrov insisted on Moscow's terms for Ukraine negotiations as the U.S. called for an immediate ceasefire at the UN Security Council.

top.title: Mamdani-backed candidates sweep three NYC congressional primaries
top.description: The New York mayor's progressive slate toppled incumbents and establishment Democrats in Tuesday's primary races across the city.

top.title: Kim commissions North Korea's largest warship, pledges nuclear navy
top.description: The 5,000-ton destroyer Choe Hyon entered service Tuesday after 14 months of testing, as Kim outlined plans for 10,000-ton warships.

top.title: Federal judge bars ICE arrests at immigration courts nationwide
top.description: The nationwide injunction vacates Trump-era policies and reinstates Biden-era limits on courthouse arrests and short-term detention.

top.title: Alan Wilson wins South Carolina GOP governor runoff
top.description: Wilson defeated Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette by a wide margin after Trump hedged his endorsement by backing both candidates.

top.title: Meta is building a prediction markets app to rival Polymarket and Kalshi
top.description: CEO Mark Zuckerberg directed a team to develop "Arena," a standalone app that would initially use a points system instead of real money.

top.title: Trump says Reflecting Pool will be drained again for 'permanent repair' around July 4
top.description: The announcement comes as algae and peeling blue paint plague the $14 million renovation, while the White House ballroom's cost has tripled to $600 million.

top.title: DOJ, New York file dueling lawsuits over mask ban for federal agents
top.description: New York's law barring officers from wearing face coverings takes effect Friday, setting up an urgent legal clash over immigration enforcement.

top.title: Bennett says Israel smuggled Starlink into Iran
top.description: Former PM told a Jerusalem summit he initiated covert delivery of tens of thousands of satellite receivers to help Iranian protesters.

top.title: DOJ charges 455 in $6.5 billion healthcare fraud takedown
top.description: The annual crackdown includes a record number of Medicaid fraud defendants and targets schemes involving false billing, opioid distribution, and wound care fraud.

top.title: Appeals court clears Trump to expand fast-track deportations nationwide
top.description: A D.C. Circuit panel reversed a lower court's block on expedited removal, allowing deportations without hearings for undocumented immigrants found anywhere in the U.S.

top.title: US, Iran finalize deal to release $12B in frozen assets
top.description: Iran's chief negotiator says the agreement, reached in Switzerland, will release the funds in two $6 billion tranches.

top.title: Tucker Carlson says he's 'out' of the Republican Party
top.description: The conservative commentator cited the GOP's alignment with Israel and support for the Iran war as reasons he can no longer back the party.

top.title: Supreme Court lets border agents strip green card holders' status over unproven charges
top.description: Justice Jackson warned the 6-3 ruling hands the government a "massive blank check" to put returning legal residents into immigration limbo.

top.title: Trump Jr. met Adani in India before DOJ dropped charges
top.description: Bloomberg reported that the previously undisclosed meeting took place in Ahmedabad last November, while Adani faced U.S. bribery charges later dismissed in May.

top.title: Mexico seeks to restart oil shipments to Cuba amid crisis
top.description: President Sheinbaum said Mexico will work to resume fuel deliveries as Cuba faces a humanitarian emergency with hospitals and schools shutting down.

top.title: Supreme Court sides with Cisco, ends Falun Gong suit
top.description: The 6-3 ruling bars federal courts from creating new causes of action under the Alien Tort Statute for international human rights violations.

top.title: ODNI firings begin as Democrats warn acting DNI Pulte
top.description: CNN reported Monday that layoffs at the intelligence office started even as top lawmakers cautioned Pulte against sweeping personnel cuts.

top.title: Coast Guard helicopter crashes near Sitka, Alaska
top.description: All four crew members were transported to a local hospital; the extent of their injuries is not yet known.

top.title: Supreme Court bars Rastafarian from suing guards who shaved his dreadlocks
top.description: The 6-3 ruling found that a federal religious rights law does not allow prisoners to seek money damages from state officials personally.

top.title: US Treasury issues 60-day license for Iranian oil sales
top.description: The sweeping sanctions waiver permits production, delivery, and U.S. import of Iranian crude through August 21 as part of a broader deal framework.

top.title: Starmer resigns as UK prime minister after two years
top.description: Andy Burnham, who won a special election last week, is the frontrunner to succeed him as Labour leader and PM.

top.title: Judge blocks Trump's use of SAVE database for voter checks
top.description: The ruling halts a key pillar of Trump's election integrity push days before a June 30 deadline to begin sharing citizenship data with states.

top.title: Judge blocks DOJ subpoenas targeting Walz over immigration
top.description: U.S. District Judge Patrick Schiltz ruled the grand jury subpoenas were issued to "harass and retaliate" against Minnesota officials who refused to aid federal immigration enforcement.

top.title: Alan Greenspan, Fed chairman for 19 years, dies at 100
top.description: His wife, Andrea Mitchell, said he died Monday morning from complications of Parkinson's disease at their home.

top.title: China sanctions 56 US firms over Pentagon blacklistings
top.description: Beijing placed 10 American companies on its export control list and barred 46 others from government procurement in retaliation.

top.title: Supreme Court reinstates murder conviction in Etan Patz case
top.description: The 6-3 ruling ends years of legal uncertainty over the 1979 disappearance that reshaped the missing-children movement in America.

top.title: US, Canada, Mexico set July 1 trilateral meeting to review USMCA
top.description: The first formal three-way review session comes as Trump has said he is "not interested in renewing" the trade pact he negotiated during his first term.

top.title: EU leaders commit to 'decisively' ramping up defense by 2030
top.description: The Brussels summit also saw clashes over the bloc's long-term budget and debate on tougher trade measures against China.

top.title: At least 30 die in Congo displacement camp as Ebola spreads undetected
top.description: Families in the Kigonze camp have refused Ebola testing on patients and the deceased, hampering efforts to contain the Bundibugyo strain outbreak.

top.title: Trump says Cuba operation could mirror Venezuela raid
top.description: The president told Axios that action against Havana is "possible" while praising Secretary of State Rubio's role in talks with Cuba.

top.title: Israeli envoy clashes with UN officials over child violations report
top.description: A record 38,558 grave violations against children in conflict were verified in 2025, with government forces identified as the main perpetrators for the first time.

top.title: Iran eyes $105B annual oil windfall as US deal lifts export barriers
top.description: Tehran has also laid groundwork to charge transit fees on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz once a 60-day grace period ends.

top.title: Trump unveils Qatar-gifted jet as new Air Force One
top.description: The aircraft was shown at Joint Base Andrews hours after the iconic VC-25A completed its final presidential mission following 35 years of service.

top.title: Iran agrees to invite IAEA to inspect nuclear sites, Witkoff tells Congress
top.description: U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff briefed lawmakers Thursday that Tehran will also allow American nuclear inspectors to visit under the deal signed this week.

top.title: Vance warns Israel not to alienate its 'only powerful ally' over Iran deal
top.description: The vice president told Israeli cabinet critics to "wake up and smell the reality" as Lebanon emerges as a flashpoint in the U.S.-Iran agreement.

top.title: Macron says Trump changed his mind on Ukraine after G7
top.description: The French president said Trump arrived at the Evian summit believing Ukraine would lose but left with a shifted assessment.

top.title: Supreme Court rules marijuana users can own guns
top.description: The justices sided with a Texas man who challenged a federal law barring drug users from possessing firearms as a Second Amendment violation.

top.title: EU pushes for role in US-Iran talks after MOU signing
top.description: The 14-point memorandum of understanding starts a 60-day clock for nuclear negotiations, but Europe has no formal seat at the table.

top.title: Trump open to phased approach on North Korea, Lee says
top.description: South Korea's president said Trump agreed to consider prioritizing a nuclear freeze over immediate denuclearization after their G7 talks.

top.title: Hegseth announces six-month review of U.S. forces in Europe
top.description: The defense secretary criticized NATO allies for denying base access during Iran operations and demanded Europe take primary responsibility for its own defense.

top.title: NASA partners with Eric Schmidt's Relativity Space on 2028 Mars orbiter
top.description: The Aeolus mission will pair NASA atmospheric instruments with Relativity Space's Terran R rocket to study Martian winds, dust, and climate.

top.title: Israel calls Trump's Iran deal a 'catastrophic capitulation'
top.description: Analysts and officials across the political spectrum condemned the 14-point memorandum signed at Versailles, saying it leaves Iran's nuclear and missile programs unaddressed.

top.title: Georgia GOP scraps redistricting plans ahead of special session
top.description: House Republicans cited pending litigation and pulled map-redrawing from Wednesday's agenda, fearing it would energize Democrats before November.

top.title: Gilgo Beach killer sentenced to life without parole
top.description: Judge called Rex Heuermann "a disgusting, small man" and "a coward" before ordering him removed from the courtroom to cheers.

top.title: ICE to sell or give away 7 detention warehouses it bought for $700M
top.description: New DHS Secretary Markwayne Mullin is reversing Kristi Noem's mega-detention strategy, shifting to existing facilities amid legal challenges and overspending scrutiny.

top.title: Von der Leyen signals no third term as EU chief
top.description: Her chief of cabinet shared the message at a private dinner to quell concerns about a planned restructuring of Commission departments.

top.title: G7 summit wraps up with pledges on debt, Ebola, development reform
top.description: Leaders in Évian-les-Bains issued joint declarations urging a shift from aid dependency to private investment and a coordinated Ebola response.

top.title: Trump vows to block FISA unless voting bill attached
top.description: Speaking at the G7 summit, the president demanded Congress link his SAVE America Act to the lapsed surveillance law renewal.

top.title: Leak reveals members of Thiel's secretive Dialog society
top.description: Internal records exposed online identify over 200 powerful figures in politics, tech, and finance tied to the invite-only network.

top.title: Brazil's top court convicts Eduardo Bolsonaro for coercion
top.description: The former lawmaker was sentenced to over four years in prison for lobbying the U.S. to intervene in his father's coup trial.

top.title: Senate blocks bid to overturn Trump immigration appeals rule
top.description: Senators voted 46-48 to reject a procedural motion on the resolution, leaving the fast-track deportation appeals process in place.

top.title: Federal judge blocks Idaho transgender bathroom law before July 1 start
top.description: Chief U.S. District Judge Amanda Brailsford ruled the law is likely unconstitutionally vague, halting enforcement statewide.

top.title: Russian frigate fires warning shots at yacht in English Channel
top.description: The UK Ministry of Defence is investigating after the Admiral Grigorovich discharged rounds near a civilian vessel south of the Isle of Wight.

top.title: Key GOP swing vote signals optimism on Blanche AG pick
top.description: Sen. Tillis, seen as the critical vote on the Judiciary Committee, said he is "generally satisfied" with the acting attorney general's paperwork.

top.title: 15 charged in Minnesota for allegedly impeding ICE agents
top.description: Prosecutors say the defendants stalked agents, threw ice at convoys, and blockaded federal buildings during a major immigration crackdown.

top.title: Pew: 52% of two-parent families now have both working full time
top.description: Two-thirds of working mothers say they cannot give 100% at home, and over half say the same about work, a new survey finds.

top.title: Trump shifts special ed, civil rights out of Education Dept.
top.description: The Office of Special Education moves to HHS and the Office for Civil Rights to the DOJ, furthering efforts to dismantle the department.

top.title: FBI foils alleged drone attack plot targeting White House UFC event
top.description: Five suspects are in custody after authorities say they disrupted a multi-phase explosive drone scheme aimed at the UFC Freedom 250 card.

top.title: SpaceX to buy Cursor maker Anysphere for $60B
top.description: Days after its record Nasdaq IPO, SpaceX said it will exercise its option to acquire the AI coding startup in an all-stock deal.

top.title: Trump invokes Defense Production Act to boost munitions output
top.description: The June 11 memo, made public Tuesday, directs the Pentagon to compel manufacturers to prioritize weapons production amid the Iran conflict.

top.title: DOJ calls xAI vital to national security in bid to dismiss NAACP pollution suit
top.description: The Justice Department argued Monday that xAI's unpermitted gas turbines in Mississippi support military operations and should not be shut down.

top.title: Israel vows to hold Lebanon territory indefinitely, defying US-Iran peace deal
top.description: Defense Minister Katz said Israeli forces will not withdraw from seized lands in Lebanon, Syria, or Gaza, challenging the framework agreement.

top.title: Thune demands briefing on Iran deal before Geneva signing
top.description: Senate Republicans are withholding support for Trump's framework agreement with Iran, insisting Congress review and vote on the deal.

top.title: Skydiving plane crash kills all 12 on board in Missouri
top.description: The plane had just taken off from Butler Memorial Airport when it lost power, stalled, and went down nose-first, the acting airport manager said.

top.title: Sweden scrambles jets to intercept Russian aircraft twice in one day
top.description: The Friday interceptions over the Baltic Sea came a day after Sweden's defense commission warned Russia may test NATO's cohesion soon.

top.title: Trump cabinet allies push kratom agenda amid financial ties, NYT details
top.description: Two Trump cabinet members advanced kratom industry interests while one held up to $1 million in a kratom company, the Times reported.

top.title: UN reports Ukraine's May civilian toll highest in 4 years
top.description: At least 274 civilians were killed and 1,763 injured, a 93% increase over May 2025, according to UN monitors.

top.title: UAE agreed to unlock billions for Iran, sources say
top.description: Reuters reported that the UAE agreed to release up to $10 billion to halt Iranian attacks, though the UAE categorically denied the claims.

top.title: Trump White House secretly weighed suspending habeas corpus, book reveals
top.description: Secret memos show officials debated limiting immigrants' habeas corpus rights more extensively than previously known, according to a New York Times report.

top.title: State Attorneys General open multistate investigation into OpenAI
top.description: New York's attorney general served OpenAI with a sweeping subpoena on behalf of a coalition of states, targeting user data practices and impact on minors.

top.title: DOJ approves Paramount's $111 billion Warner Bros. Discovery merger
top.description: The deal cleared federal antitrust review without any required divestitures or concessions, though state attorneys general may still sue to block it.

top.title: Trump aides fear reporters obtained Situation Room tapes
top.description: Axios reports White House officials suspect Haberman and Swan acquired secret audio from sensitive meetings for their book "Regime Change," due June 23.

top.title: Elon Musk becomes the world’s first trillionaire after SpaceX IPO
top.description: The rocket and satellite company raised $75 billion by selling shares at $135 each, valuing SpaceX at $1.77 trillion at its Nasdaq debut.

top.title: EU says it has proof China trained Russian troops fighting in Ukraine
top.description: European intelligence confirmed that hundreds of Russian soldiers trained at Chinese military bases before deploying to the front lines.

top.title: Trump marks 80th birthday with UFC fights on White House lawn
top.description: The president hosts UFC Freedom 250 on the South Lawn as polls show growing public concern over his health and mental acuity.

top.title: Pentagon releases third batch of declassified UFO files
top.description: The latest tranche includes 72 documents, videos, and NASA audio recordings spanning back to 1949, with new firsthand accounts of luminous "orbs."

top.title: Bezos and Amodei offer clashing visions of AI's job impact
top.description: AI-linked U.S. layoffs hit a record monthly high in May even as Bezos predicts the technology will create labor shortages, not eliminate jobs.

top.title: Charlie Javice seeks Trump pardon for JPMorgan fraud conviction
top.description: The Frank founder, sentenced to 85 months for fabricating customer data to sell her startup for $175 million, has reached out to Trump allies.

top.title: U.S. plans to cut fighter jets, warships for NATO in Europe
top.description: A New York Times report details sweeping reductions to aircraft, submarines, and carriers available for European defense operations.

top.title: Bezos' AI startup Prometheus raises $12B at $41B valuation
top.description: The industrial AI company closed its Series B backed by JPMorgan, BlackRock, and Goldman Sachs, bringing total funding to $18.2 billion.

top.title: Trump admin blocks foreign access to Anthropic's Fable 5, Mythos 5
top.description: Commerce Secretary Lutnick ordered export restrictions on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5, barring all foreign users days after the models launched.

top.title: Supreme Court blocks Alabama nitrogen gas execution
top.description: The 6-3 ruling upheld a lower court's finding that nitrogen hypoxia violates the Eighth Amendment's ban on cruel and unusual punishment.

top.title: ECB hikes rates for first time in 3 years as Iran war fuels inflation
top.description: The deposit rate rises to 2.25% as eurozone inflation hits 3.2%, with markets pricing in roughly even odds of another increase in September.

top.title: Trump says US strike killed Tren de Aragua leader
top.description: The president announced the killing of Niño Guerrero, who had been indicted on terrorism and racketeering charges and carried a $5 million bounty.

top.title: Boelter pleads guilty to killing Minnesota lawmaker Hortman
top.description: The plea deal calls for two life sentences plus 40 years after federal prosecutors agreed not to seek the death penalty.

top.title: OpenAI considers drastic token price cuts to fend off Anthropic
top.description: The move, reported by The Wall Street Journal, comes as both AI giants prepare for IPOs and enterprise customers push back on soaring costs.

top.title: Trump and allies push to void his two impeachments
top.description: The effort to formally expunge both first-term House impeachment votes from the record would wait until after the November midterm elections.

top.title: Pentagon locked down after air quality alert triggers hazmat response
top.description: Emergency crews in full chemical protective gear responded Thursday after internal systems flagged an air quality concern at the military headquarters.

top.title: IAEA board passes resolution demanding Iran account for uranium stockpile
top.description: The U.S.-backed measure requires Tehran to disclose the status of enriched uranium at sites bombed during last year's conflict and grant inspectors access.

top.title: Russia's war in Ukraine surpasses World War I in duration
top.description: The full-scale invasion reached 1,569 days on Wednesday, outlasting the First World War as the Trump administration's June peace deadline passed without resolution.

top.title: Hegseth warns Cuba against acquiring weapons during Guantanamo visit
top.description: The defense secretary told Havana that seeking arms capable of reaching the base or the U.S. homeland would invite a confrontation it "could not withstand."

top.title: Gates tells Congress Epstein tried to blackmail him
top.description: The Microsoft co-founder testified before the House Oversight Committee, saying Epstein used knowledge of his affairs to pressure him.

top.title: Trump administration appeals ruling that struck down $100,000 H-1B visa fee
top.description: The Justice Department filed a notice of appeal Thursday, escalating a legal fight over the fee a federal judge called an unlawful tax.

top.title: US strikes destroy water reservoirs in Iran, drawing war crime accusations
top.description: Iran says US airstrikes on Sirik cut drinking water to thousands, as both sides trade fire in a second day of renewed hostilities.

top.title: US consumer prices rose 4.2% in May, fastest pace in 3 years
top.description: The Iran war-driven energy surge pushed headline CPI past 4% for the first time since 2023, though a softer core reading eased fears of broader inflation.

top.title: White House defends Cuba sanctions after UN warns children are dying
top.description: The Trump administration said sanctions target the Cuban regime, rejecting UN calls to lift measures that officials say are causing "widespread harm."

top.title: IRGC claims drone strike on US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain
top.description: Iran says it attacked the US naval headquarters hours after American strikes hit Iranian military sites in a rapid cycle of retaliation.

top.title: Fired Navy admiral advances to runoff for Mace's SC House seat
top.description: Nancy Lacore, dismissed by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, will face Mac Deford in a June 23 Democratic runoff for South Carolina's 1st District.

top.title: Trump claims US secretly took oil from Iran; Energy Secretary says he was unaware
top.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright told Congress he had no knowledge of the operation Trump described as extracting "millions of barrels every night."

top.title: House passes Faster Labor Contracts Act as 20 Republicans break ranks
top.description: The bipartisan bill would force employers to begin union contract talks within 10 days and impose binding arbitration if negotiations stall.

top.title: Platner wins Maine Democratic Senate primary, will face Collins in fall
top.description: The scandal-plagued oyster farmer and veteran secured the nomination decisively, setting up a marquee race for Senate control.

top.title: Trump signs $70B Secure America Act funding ICE, CBP
top.description: The law locks in immigration enforcement funding through 2029, ending a months-long standoff that triggered the longest government shutdown in U.S. history.

top.title: Belfast rocked by riots after Sudanese man charged in stabbing
top.description: Masked protesters torched vehicles and blocked roads Tuesday night as the suspect, a refugee granted UK asylum in 2023, was charged with attempted murder.

top.title: Anthropic launches Claude Fable 5, its first public Mythos-class model
top.description: The AI model uses automatic safety classifiers that route sensitive queries to a less powerful system, with mandatory 30-day data retention.

top.title: Becerra, Hilton advance to California governor race
top.description: Republican Steve Hilton clinched the second spot Tuesday, setting up a November showdown with Democrat Xavier Becerra to succeed Gov. Gavin Newsom.

top.title: US warns Europe to tighten Ebola travel curbs or face restrictions
top.description: The Trump administration says Europe's inaction on Ebola screening could trigger limits on European travelers ahead of the World Cup.

top.title: Social Security trust fund now projected to run dry in 2032
top.description: The 2026 trustees report moves the OASI depletion date up a year, meaning retirees could face a 22% benefit cut as early as late 2032.

top.title: NASA names four astronauts for Artemis III lunar mission
top.description: Bresnik, Parmitano, Douglas, and Rubio will fly a 2027 Earth-orbit test flight critical to returning humans to the moon.

top.title: MAGA Inc. plans $1M-per-person fundraiser before White House UFC event
top.description: Trump's super PAC is scheduled to hold the dinner Saturday at his Virginia golf club, one day before UFC Freedom 250 on the South Lawn.

top.title: Houthis strike two ships after banning Israeli Red Sea navigation
top.description: The attacks enforce a declared ban on Israeli-linked vessels, threatening Saudi Arabia's last functioning oil export route as the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.

top.title: Israel strikes Tyre, killing 8, defying Iran warning
top.description: The IDF issued its first evacuation order covering Tyre's Christian quarter as airstrikes hit the southern Lebanese city Tuesday.

top.title: Drone boat rescues Apache crew in military first after Strait of Hormuz crash
top.description: A U.S. military sea drone conducted its first-ever water rescue after an AH-64 Apache went down off Oman's coast Monday evening.

top.title: China plans $295B data center buildout to rival U.S. in AI
top.description: Beijing is drafting a blueprint for interconnected computing hubs nationwide, funded largely through sovereign debt over the next five years.

top.title: Philippines earthquake death toll rises to 37 as rescuers search for survivors
top.description: The 7.8-magnitude quake struck off Mindanao on Monday, triggering landslides, building collapses, and tsunami waves across the region.

top.title: Watchdog finds flawed data, security gaps in IRS-ICE deal
top.description: TIGTA's report documents matching errors and ICE's failure to safeguard taxpayer information shared under the controversial 2025 agreement.

top.title: Senate Democrats seek probe into Duffy's corporate-funded road trip
top.description: Six senators asked the DOT inspector general to investigate the transportation secretary's family video series bankrolled by regulated companies.

top.title: Trump to ask Supreme Court to revive $475M CNN suit
top.description: His lawyers requested a 60-day extension to file a petition challenging lower courts' dismissal of the "Big Lie" defamation case.

top.title: State Department to offer $750 fast-track visa interviews
top.description: A pilot program launching July 1 lets B-1/B-2 visa applicants pay $750 on top of the standard $185 fee to secure an interview within 10 business days.

top.title: 6.1 earthquake off Cuba sends tremors across Florida
top.description: The quake struck around 2 p.m. Monday about 65 miles from Mantua, Cuba, with shaking reported from the Keys to Jacksonville.
